summary: Iteration 5 — async submission refactor verified end-to-end. Backend: 12/12 pytest cases pass in 60.7s (test_iter5_async_submissions.py). POST /api/submissions returns in well under 5s (measured ~0.4-1.0s) with status='pending', photos_total=N, photos_done=0, pdf_available=false. Polling GET shows pending→processing→completed; pdf_available flips to true only after the last photo and PDF generation completes. Final Plus submission has all results.after as non-empty base64 PNGs (>1KB) and GET /api/submissions/{id}/pdf returns application/pdf >5KB. Unknown id returns 404 on both /api/submissions/{id} and /api/submissions/{id}/pdf. Regression on /api/plans and /api/checkout/session (plus+premium accepted, free rejected) still passes. Frontend e2e on /upload/plus: filled email + 'bedroom' select + 1x1 PNG → Transform → budget modal → '$100 – $300' → 'Confirm & generate my plan' → navigated to /result/{id} in ~2s → progress card visible (data-testid='result-progress-card', pct '0%' → '85%') → completion observed at ~40s → before/after slider rendered, 'Download My Design Plan' button visible. /result/non-existent-id-test renders the exact copy 'We couldn\'t find that submission. It may have expired.' inside [data-testid=result-error]. Landing-page regression: cluttered_garage/closet/laundry images all present, 5 FAQ items, 4 gallery filter buttons. All review_request criteria met.

issues: H1 heading reads 'Loading…' while the red error 'We couldn\'t find that submission…' is shown below — heading should switch to e.g. 'We couldn\'t find that.' when err is set so the page state is consistent. Minor UX nit.

action_items: (Optional) Tweak Result.jsx h1 to render an error-specific heading when err is set instead of 'Loading…'.

(Optional, perf) generate_after_image is called sequentially inside _process_submission. Premium (4 photos) will take ~2-4 min wall-clock. Consider asyncio.gather with a semaphore=2 to cut latency while protecting GPT Image 1 rate limits.
(Optional, robustness) Add a watchdog/cleanup task: if a 'pending' submission stays untouched for >10 min (e.g., container restart), mark it 'failed' so the UI stops polling forever.
critical_code_review_comments: server.py:555 uses asyncio.create_task(...) without keeping a strong reference. CPython may GC the task — see PEP 661 / asyncio docs. Recommend storing tasks in a module-level set and adding done-callback for removal, OR using FastAPI BackgroundTasks. In practice uvicorn keeps a strong reference internally for now but this is a known footgun.
server.py:_process_submission: if the backend process restarts mid-processing, the submission is stuck in 'processing' forever — no resume/timeout logic. Add a TTL or status reaper.

server.py:548 — initial doc stores full base64 before-images in Mongo immediately; for Premium with 4 photos that can exceed Mongo's 16MB doc limit once 'after' images are appended. Plan to move binaries to GridFS or object storage.
Result.jsx:33 — when MAX_WAIT_MS (8 min) elapses without completion, polling stops silently but UI stays on 'Working on your transformation…' indefinitely. Show a soft-timeout fallback message.
Result.jsx h1 condition cascade doesn't account for `err` state — when error is set, h1 still shows 'Loading…' (see design_issues above).
test_credentials: n/a — no auth in this app.
seed_data_creation: Created real submissions in Mongo with TEST_iter5_* prefixed emails (free, plus). Each consumed a real GPT Image 1 generation + Claude PDF design plan. Plus submission generated a >5KB PDF.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Iter5 verified the async submission refactor. POST returns in ~0.4-1.0s with status='pending'. Background asyncio.create_task runs _process_submission which sequentially generates per-photo AI 'after' images (updating photos_done after each) and finally renders the PDF for paid plans. Result.jsx polls every 3.5s. Hot reload picks up server.py edits automatically. Test fixture uses load_dotenv on /app/frontend/.env to fetch REACT_APP_BACKEND_URL. The full Plus 1-photo flow completes in ~30-45s wall-clock; use TIMEOUT >= 240s for tests. Notable code review items above (esp. asyncio.create_task strong-ref + missing watchdog for stuck 'processing' docs) are not blocking iter5 but should be queued.
